LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT ADVANTA MORTGAGE CORP. U.S.A. AND ADVANTA MORTGAGE CONDUIT SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES ON APPEAL. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED AWARDING ATTORNEYS FEES OF $348,736 AND COSTS OF $12,136.61. THE COURT HAS REDUCED THE REQUEST AS FOLLOWS: REVIEW OF NOTICE OF APPEAL: REDUCE BIDERMAN TIME BY 2 HOURS AT $320 AND KLINGES TIME BY 3 HOURS AT $500 = $2140. MOTION TO POST A BOND: REDUCE BIDERMAN TIME BY 15 HOURS AND KLINGES TIME BY 6 HOURS; $7800. NO REDUCTION IS MADE FOR THE WRIT OR THE BRIEF ON APPEAL GIVEN THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE ISSUES AND THE COMPLEXITY OF THE CASE. PREPARATION FOR ORAL ARGUMENT: THE ARGUMENT WAS MADE BY KRABIL; THE TIME OF BIDERMAN IS REDUCED BY 20 HOURS AT $340 AND BY KRABIL $20 HOURS AT $450 = $15,800. THE REQUEST OF $374,476 IS REDUCED BY $25,740 AND THE AMOUNT AWARDED IS $348,736. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE HOURLY RATES ARE CONSISTENT WITH MARKET RATES FOR COMPLEX LITIGATION OF THE KIND PRESENTED BY THIS CASE AND THE WORK OF THE HOWARD RICE FIRM, ACTING AS LOCAL COUNSEL, WAS NECESSARY AND REASONABLE. Off Calendar May-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT ADVANTA MORTGAGE CORP. U.S.A. AND ADVANTA MORTGAGE CONDUIT SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES ON APPEAL.
APPEARANCES: LOUIS H. CASTORIA, ESQUIRE (WILSON ELSER, 415.433.0990) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. DANA B. KLINGES, ESQUIRE (WOLF BLOCK, 215.977.2632) AND AMY E. MARGOLIN, ESQUIRE (HOWARD RICE, ET AL., 415.677.3401) APPEARING FOR RESPONDENT ADVANTA
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: granted awarding attorneys fees of $348,736 and costs of $12,136.61.
The Court has reduced the request as follows:
Review of notice of appeal: reduce Biderman time by 2 hours at $320 and Klinges time by 3 hours at $500 = $2140
Motion to post a bond: reduce Biderman time by 15 hours and Klinges time by 6 hours; $7800
No reduction is made for the writ or the brief on appeal given the significance of the issues and the complexity of the case.
Preparation for oral argument: the argument was made by Krabil; the time of Biderman is reduced by 20 hours at $340 and by Krabil $20 hours at $450 = $15,800
The request of $374,476 is reduced by $25,740 and the amount awarded is $348,736.
The Court finds that the hourly rates are consistent with market rates for complex litigation of the kind presented by this case and the work of the Howard Rice firm, acting as local counsel, was necessary and reasonable. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
